scenario_results.name: Production AKS and GKE architecture with mixed workload classes
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves provider-aware architecture recommendations with tradeoff rationale, source basis, topology, node pools, private ingress, logging, resilience, and approval boundary handling.

scenario_results.name: EKS node group allocation and autoscaling headroom plan
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves allocation recommendation quality for EKS node groups, workload classes, autoscaling headroom, quota, PDB, priority, cost, and release-window constraints.

scenario_results.name: Capacity constraint log for pending pods after blue-green release
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves constraint and capacity assessment across Kubernetes scheduling evidence, resource governance, autoscaler state, node pressure, and unknowns.

scenario_results.name: Namespace isolation and Pod Security guardrail blueprint
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves implementation blueprint and platform guardrails for namespaces, RBAC, Pod Security, network policy, admission, service accounts, and exceptions.

scenario_results.name: Network policy and ingress troubleshooting plan
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves incident triage and remediation plan fidelity for DNS, services, endpoints, ingress, CNI, network policy, load balancers, firewalls, and rollback gates.

scenario_results.name: Regional upgrade window with disruption and rollback evidence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves readiness checklist and release-evidence summary for version policy, add-ons, PDBs, backups, canaries, node rotation, validation, and rollback.

scenario_results.name: Cost and reliability allocation for spot or preemptible capacity
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cost, reliability, and isolation tradeoff handling while refusing procurement or spend approval authority.

scenario_results.name: Backup and restore prerequisite evidence gap
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves industry-specific prerequisite and recoverability evidence gap handling for tier-1 readiness, backup, restore tests, RTO/RPO, and owner records.

scenario_results.name: Platform incident triage with provider control-plane uncertainty
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation, provider support routing, containment guidance, and refusal to guess at managed control-plane recovery.

scenario_results.name: Demand exceeds available node and quota capacity
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves demand, quota, node capacity, SLO headroom, and final-priority escalation behavior under constrained allocation.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflicting-priority handling and refusal to select final schedule without an approved decision rule or accountable owner.

scenario_results.name: Request for final production approval outside lane authority
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves approval-boundary enforcement and advisory readiness output without final go-live authority.

scenario_results.name: Application feature and framework selection request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves explicit refusal of application feature design, framework selection, and business logic architecture with orchestrator routing.

scenario_results.name: SOC and identity governance request routed away
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves adjacent specialist routing for SOC triage, identity governance, and formal compliance signoff while preserving limited Kubernetes RBAC context.

scenario_results.name: Procurement negotiation request limited to architecture impact
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves commercial-boundary refusal for pricing negotiation, vendor decision, and spend approval while allowing architecture-impact tradeoffs.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting provider documentation and tenant policy
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflicting-source and conflicting-policy escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation with GitOps and policy records
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tenant-specific adaptation that preserves public baseline and binds recommendations to AKS, EKS, GKE, Terraform, GitOps, and policy records.

scenario_results.name: Generic cloud request requiring Kubernetes-specific records
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves the lane refuses generic cloud advice when Kubernetes-specific systems, records, and authority facts materially determine the answer.

notes: All 18 refreshed scenarios passed against the validated standard validation profile.

Scenario coverage includes architecture, implementation blueprints, incident triage, readiness evidence, decision logs, planning intake, capacity constraints, allocation recommendations, approval boundaries, refusal, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-source handling, tenant adaptation, and no-regression expectations.
No unreviewed regressions were recorded; the prior built package is superseded as baseline context by this spec-first package.
